cmd.root.description:
MindX is an AI-native multi-agent conversation platform.

Default behavior:
  Running 'mindx' starts the TUI (Terminal UI) for chatting.

Commands:
  daemon   Run the daemon process (background server)
  start    Start the installed daemon service
  stop     Stop the Daemon service
  install  Install to system (PATH + Daemon + shortcuts)
  status   Show system status
  doctor   Diagnose system health
  logs     View or tail Daemon logs
  version  Print version and build info
  query    Search long-term memory
  provider Manage LLM providers (list / rm / add)
  model    Manage LLM models (list / rm / add)
  agent    Manage AI agents (list / rm / add)
  web      Open browser to access WebUI

Examples:
  mindx                    # Start TUI chat interface
  mindx daemon           # Run daemon process
  mindx daemon --port 8080 # Specify port
  mindx start             # Start daemon via service manager
  mindx install           # Install to system
  mindx status            # Show status
  mindx logs              # View logs
  mindx logs -n 100 -f    # Tail logs in real-time
  mindx version           # Show version
  mindx query "term"      # Search long-term memory
  mindx provider list    # List providers
  mindx model list       # List models
  mindx model set gpt-4  # Set default model
  mindx agent list       # List agents
  mindx doctor            # Diagnose health
  mindx doctor --fix      # Auto-fix issues
  mindx web               # Open WebUI
cmd.root.hint.install: Hint: Run 'mindx install' to install MindX to your system (PATH / Daemon / shortcuts)
cmd.root.firstRun.detected: First run detected, entering setup wizard...

cmd.daemon.short: Run the MindX daemon process (background server)
cmd.daemon.long:
Run the MindX daemon process, providing WebSocket gateway and HTTP service.

This is the actual server process. It is normally launched by the system service manager via 'mindx start'.

For WebUI, MacUI or other client connections.
cmd.daemon.error.notconfigured: Initial configuration not completed. Please run 'mindx' first to enter setup wizard.
cmd.start.short: Start the installed MindX daemon service
cmd.web.long:
Open MindX WebUI in default browser.

Requires the daemon running (use 'mindx start' first).
cmd.web.hint.daemon.required: Daemon is not running. Please run 'mindx start' first.
cmd.skill.long:
List or inspect installed MindX skills.

Skills are located in ~/.mindx/skills/ — each is a directory with a SKILL.md file.
cmd.skill.get.short: Show details of a specific skill
cmd.rule.long:
List or inspect permission rules for tool access control.

Permission rules define which tools are always allowed, always denied, or require user confirmation.
cmd.rule.get.short: Show details of a specific permission rule
cmd.schedule.long:
List, add, or delete scheduled tasks.

Scheduled tasks run automatically on a cron schedule via the MindX daemon. Requires the daemon to be restarted after adding or deleting schedules.

wizard.daemon.notinstalled.desc:
⚙️ Daemon Service

🔴 **Not Installed**

Daemon is the core service of MindX, providing multi-Agent collaboration and Web UI.
If skipped, only TUI will be available.

Local chat works without it, but these features won't be available:
  - Multi-Agent orchestration
  - WebSocket remote connection
  - System tray resident mode

Register as auto-start service?
wizard.python.ready.format:
🐍 Python Environment

✅ **Python %s · Virtual Environment Ready**

Virtual environment isolates Python dependencies. Skills system works normally.

**Enter** Continue  **S** Skip
wizard.python.detected.no.venv.format:
🐍 Python Environment

🟢 **Python %s** Detected
🔴 **No Virtual Environment**

Virtual environment isolates Python dependencies required by skills.
After creation, all requirements.txt under skills/ will be installed.
Without it, Python skills won't work but core chat functions normally.

Create virtual environment?

%s

← → Toggle  **Enter** Confirm  **Esc** Exit
wizard.python.notinstalled.desc:
🐍 Python Environment

🔴 **Python Not Installed**

Python is a required component. Skills system depends on Python.

After configuration, Python 3.12 will be automatically installed.

You can also manually install:

  1. Visit python.org to download Python 3.10+
  2. Check "Add Python to PATH" during installation
  3. Re-run the setup wizard when done

**Enter** Continue  **Esc** Skip  **q** Quit
wizard.memory.embedder.ready.detail:
💾 Memory Configuration

✅ **Embedder Model Embedded**

Chinese-CLIP (model_q4.onnx) is bundled internally,
released to workspace on startup. No separate download needed.

Memory features are enabled by default, supporting semantic search and RAG cross-session retrieval.

**Enter** Continue  **S** Skip

**Enter** Continue  **S** Skip

📌 System PATH Configuration

Install directory: %s

After adding mindx's directory to the system PATH environment variable, you can run mindx from any terminal window.
(Modifies user-level PATH, no admin rights needed)

Add to PATH?

%s
